why_tt_fails: The _matrix_fold_dims for d=3 creates mode-0 dimension of only 32 (from m=2048 → [32,64])
First SVD is on (32, 98304) — max 32 singular values available, matrix has 1536 DoF
Auto-computed rank r=23 keeps only 23/32 singular values from first mode
Second SVD is on (23*64, 1536) = (1472, 1536) — rank-23 out of 1472 possible, SV ratio = 505
Result: TT captures only ~14% of signal variance (rel_mse=0.86, SNR=0.65dB)
why_subsequent_stages_fail: TT residual looks like random noise (no exploitable structure)
Sparse top-1% captures SNR=0.02dB of TT residual
Ergodic sine fitting captures SNR=0.00dB of sparse residual
SIREN (from vision tower test) captures SNR=0.00dB of ergodic residual
All post-TT stages combined improve SNR from 0.65→0.66dB — effectively zero value

contribution_breakdown.TT-SVD rank truncation: 86.1% of original variance lost (rel_mse=0.861 in permuted space)
contribution_breakdown.SIREN: 0% improvement (200-epoch SIREN final loss = initial loss)

recommendations: CRITICAL: Fix TT-SVD. Use d=5 instead of d=3 to avoid mode-0 bottleneck.
CRITICAL: Remove automatic rank computation from target_ratio. Set minimum rank = max(128, 2*all_dims[0]).
HIGH: Skip stages 3-5 entirely when TT error dominates. Add a variance-aware gate.
HIGH: Replace TT-SVD with full SVD + low-rank for matrices smaller than 4096×4096.
MEDIUM: If keeping residual stages, use quantized residual instead of sparse/ergodic for noise-like residuals.
LOW: SIREN is useless — 200 epochs of gradient descent cannot fit a near-noise tensor.

ablation_summary.TT-SVD rank-64 only: SNR = 1.20 dB (still terrible — mode-0 limited to 31)
